tossups.question: A planned extension of this project to the Virgin Islands was designed to “root like a cactus to a rocky peninsula.” A 2018 exhibition at the Chicago Architecture Biennial documented an abandoned clone of this project intended for San Juan’s Hato Rey neighborhood that conformed to Title 236 requirements. This building was constructed after Sandy Van Ginkel convinced his student to submit his master’s thesis to a design contest, although it was reduced from a planned 1,000 (*) units. The book For Everyone a Garden documents the architect’s extension of this project to his native Israel. This building is made up of 354 stacked concrete boxes that form 158 apartments and was built for a “Man and His World”-themed event along the Saint Lawrence River. For 10 points, name this building created for the Montreal Expo of 1967 by Moshe Safdie.
tossups.question_sanitized: A planned extension of this project to the Virgin Islands was designed to "root like a cactus to a rocky peninsula." A 2018 exhibition at the Chicago Architecture Biennial documented an abandoned clone of this project intended for San Juan's Hato Rey neighborhood that conformed to Title 236 requirements. This building was constructed after Sandy Van Ginkel convinced his student to submit his master's thesis to a design contest, although it was reduced from a planned 1,000 (*) units. The book For Everyone a Garden documents the architect's extension of this project to his native Israel. This building is made up of 354 stacked concrete boxes that form 158 apartments and was built for a "Man and His World"-themed event along the Saint Lawrence River. For 10 points, name this building created for the Montreal Expo of 1967 by Moshe Safdie.

tossups.question: Jerry Adamson designed special rotary-moulded polyethylene armchairs made specifically for this structure. Ricardo Bofill’s Kafka Castle strongly resembles this structure, and the construction technique used to design it inspired the similar-looking Nakagin Capsule Tower. August Komendant supervised the structural engineering of this project, whose original proposal also included shopping centers and schools and was outlined in the college (*) thesis “A Case for City Living.” Interlocking walkways and terraces on top of buildings were part of a plan for this complex, in which each roof provides another unit’s garden. This structure was built for an event whose motto was “Man and His World,” and consists of 354 identical, 90-ton, pre-fabricated “boxes” that were assembled nearby the St. Lawrence River. For 10 points, name this residential community designed for the Montreal World Expo, a housing project which started the career of Moshe Safdie.
tossups.question_sanitized: Jerry Adamson designed special rotary-moulded polyethylene armchairs made specifically for this structure. Ricardo Bofill's Kafka Castle strongly resembles this structure, and the construction technique used to design it inspired the similar-looking Nakagin Capsule Tower. August Komendant supervised the structural engineering of this project, whose original proposal also included shopping centers and schools and was outlined in the college (*) thesis "A Case for City Living." Interlocking walkways and terraces on top of buildings were part of a plan for this complex, in which each roof provides another unit's garden. This structure was built for an event whose motto was "Man and His World," and consists of 354 identical, 90-ton, pre-fabricated "boxes" that were assembled nearby the St. Lawrence River. For 10 points, name this residential community designed for the Montreal World Expo, a housing project which started the career of Moshe Safdie.

bonuses.leadin: Margaret Schütte-Lihotsky designed a kitchen with stain-resistant beech surfaces for use in one of these projects. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these projects. Schütte-Lihotsky worked on one of them spearheaded by Ernst May in Frankfurt. Moshe Safdie used concrete blocks for another of these projects built for Expo 67 in Montreal.

In a fire station for this city, Susana Torre designed individual bedrooms in an attempt to shift bonding toward the kitchen and include women socially. Robert Venturi also designed a Modernist firehouse for this Indiana city.

This architect left the kitchen visible from dining spaces, as opposed to hidden away, in his open floor plan for the Usonian Willey House. This architect integrated a waterfall into his Fallingwater.
answers: housing complexes [or public housing projects or apartment buildings; prompt on Habitat 67]
answers_sanitized: housing complexes [or public housing projects or apartment buildings; prompt on Habitat 67]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Margaret Schutte-Lihotsky designed a kitchen with stain-resistant beech surfaces for use in one of these projects. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name these projects. Schutte-Lihotsky worked on one of them spearheaded by Ernst May in Frankfurt. Moshe Safdie used concrete blocks for another of these projects built for Expo 67 in Montreal.

In a fire station for this city, Susana Torre designed individual bedrooms in an attempt to shift bonding toward the kitchen and include women socially. Robert Venturi also designed a Modernist firehouse for this Indiana city.

This architect left the kitchen visible from dining spaces, as opposed to hidden away, in his open floor plan for the Usonian Willey House. This architect integrated a waterfall into his Fallingwater.

bonuses.leadin: In 2017, Rome mayor Virginia Raggi prevented three triangular towers designed by this architect from being included in the park next to football club AS (“ah-ess”) Roma’s planned new stadium. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Polish-American architect whose Jewish Museum in Berlin has several empty “voids” lacking air conditioning, one of which contains thousands of iron cutouts resembling human faces.
David Childs of Skidmore, Owings, and Merrill discarded much of Libeskind’s preliminary design for this New York skyscraper built as a spiritual replacement to the Twin Towers destroyed on 9/11.

Libeskind’s “Crystal” was a controversial addition to a museum in this country. Moshe Safdie (“mo-SHEH SAHF-dee”) built a modular “Habitat” for a World’s Fair in this country, the home country of the architect of the Dancing House.
answers: One World Trade Center [or One WTC; or Freedom Tower; prompt on World Trade Center or WTC]
Canada (The architect of the Dancing House is Frank Gehry, and Habitat 67 was for Montreal’s Expo.)
answers_sanitized: One World Trade Center [or One WTC; or Freedom Tower; prompt on World Trade Center or WTC]
Canada (The architect of the Dancing House is Frank Gehry, and Habitat 67 was for Montreal's Expo.)
